pi_items.verified_value: 0.333 ft (4.0 inches) - plausible for extensive green roof soil layer
pi_items.source: Standard green roof specifications: extensive roofs typically 2-4 inches of growing media

pi_items.verified_value: 0.42 Btu-in/h-ft²-F (≈0.0606 W/(m·K)) - plausible for lightweight engineered growing media
pi_items.source: Web search on green roof material properties. Engineered substrates typically lower conductivity than natural soil due to air voids.

pi_items.verified_value: 75.0 lb/ft³ - within documented range for green roof growing media (54-95 lb/ft³)
pi_items.source: Columbia Green Roof and EcoRoof Systems documentation on green roof substrate density specifications

pi_items.verified_value: 0.25 Btu/lb-F - reasonable for soil-based materials (0.2-0.3 typical range)
pi_items.source: Engineering material property databases and thermal analysis standards

pi_items.verified_value: 0.042 ft (≈0.5 inches) - plausible for thin drainage mat or geotextile layer
pi_items.source: Green roof design standards: drainage layer typically 10mm-50mm (0.39-1.97 inches), this is at thin end

pi_items.verified_value: 0.03 Btu-in/h-ft²-F (≈0.00433 W/(m·K)) - very low, consistent with foam-based drainage mat
pi_items.source: Polyethylene and polypropylene foam materials documentation: low thermal conductivity foams

pi_items.verified_value: 2.5 lb/ft³ - very light, at lower end for foam materials (~1-2 lb/ft³ typical for expanded foam)
pi_items.source: Foam insulation material specifications and polyethylene foam products

pi_items.verified_value: 0.35 Btu/lb-F - reasonable for plastic foam materials
0.333 ft (4.0 inches) - standard thickness for foam board insulation
pi_items.source: Building insulation product specifications and standards

pi_items.verified_value: For claimed thickness 0.333 ft (4 inches) and conductivity 0.017 Btu-in/h-ft²-F, actual R-value = 4 in / 0.017 = 235.1 (NOT 20)
pi_items.source: R-value calculation: R = thickness(inches) / conductivity(Btu-in/h-ft²-F). For true R-20 with 4 inches, conductivity should be 0.2 Btu-in/h-ft²-F

pi_items.verified_value: 1.5 lb/ft³ - reasonable for foam board insulation (XPS/EPS typically 1.5-2.0 lb/ft³)
pi_items.source: Extruded polystyrene and foam board specifications

pi_items.verified_value: 0.25 Btu/lb-F - reasonable for plastic foam materials
Green-Roof-Assembly - consistent with task description and verifier expectations

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real 4StoreyBuilding.inp model from equest_env/data, which contains 5 roof surfaces. The task requires creating three custom green roof materials with specified thermal properties and a construction assembly. Material properties are mostly plausible for green roof components based on industry standards: GR-Soil-4in (75 lb/ft³ density, 0.42 Btu-in/h-ft²-F conductivity) matches lightweight engineered growing media; GR-Drainage-Mat (2.5 lb/ft³, 0.03 conductivity) represents foam-based drainage; GR-Insulation-R20 has reasonable foam properties (1.5 lb/ft³). CRITICAL ISSUE: GR-Insulation-R20 is named 'R20' but the specified conductivity (0.017 Btu-in/h-ft²-F) with thickness (4 inches) yields R-value of 235, not 20. This indicates either a naming error or incorrect conductivity specification. The verifier checks material properties with ±1% tolerance and counts roof surface assignments.
